'''Simon Connelly Booth''' (born 5 October 1956) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly Warringah]] and [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]] in the [[New South Wales Rugby League|NSWRL]] competition.

Recruited from [[Lakes United]] in Newcastle, Booth was a centre and winger during his career.<ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article110639252 |title=Important game for New Zealand|newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=24 May 1975 |accessdate=12 October 2018 |page=37}}</ref>

Booth featured in Manly's 1978 premiership team, playing on the wing in both the grand final draw and replay win over Cronulla.{{fact|date=April 2022}}

In 1983 and 1984 he played for Balmain, under his former Manly coach [[Frank Stanton (rugby league)|Frank Stanton]].<ref>{{cite news |url=http://nla.gov.au/nla.news-article116419722 |title=Eden kicks the Saints out of business|newspaper=[[The Canberra Times]]|date=25 July 1983 |accessdate=12 October 2018 |page=13}}</ref>
